changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in Tough Tech companies commercializing breakthrough science and engineering. The firm supports early-stage deep tech startups developing hardware-based technologies across biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing.
verified.notes: Engine Ventures is the investment fund that separated from The Engine (nonprofit incubator/accelerator) in 2023. The Engine itself remains a 501(c)(3) nonprofit as of 2025. Source [3] indicates the fund 'separated from The Engine and rebranded as the venture capital firm Engine Ventures' in 2023. Current senior partners and AUM figures not explicitly stated in provided sources.

changesProposed.name: Future Ventures

verified.name: Future Ventures
verified.thesis: Future Ventures focuses on seed and early-stage investments in trailblazing, purpose-driven entrepreneurs with unique ideas that have the potential to reinvent entire industries. The firm backs visionaries at the cutting edge of disruptive technology and new industry formation.
verified.notes: Website states 'For the past 25 years' (source 0, 1) backing visionary founders, but no specific founding year provided in sources. AUM, flagship fund name, and HQ location not stated in sources. Sector focus cannot be verified from explicit sector lists on firm site—only inferred from individual investment examples. Founded year in database entry (2018) contradicts 'past 25 years' language.

changes.current: Future Ventures focuses on seed and early-stage investments in mission-driven, purpose-driven entrepreneurs with unique ideas that have the potential to reinvent entire industries. The firm backs visionaries at the cutting edge of disruptive technology and new industry formation.
